title: XML parsers should not allow inclusion of arbitrary files
description: Allowing XML parsers to include arbitrary files can lead to serious security vulnerabilities, such as disclosure of sensitive information or remote code execution. XML External Entity (XXE) attacks exploit this behavior to access files on the server or perform other malicious actions. Parsers should be configured to disallow inclusion of external files unless explicitly required and safe.
recommendation: Configure XML parsers to disable external entity resolution and disallow inclusion of arbitrary files. Use secure parser settings and validate all XML input.
impact: Attackers may gain unauthorized access to sensitive files or execute malicious code on the server.

checks.message: Configure XML parsers to disable external entity resolution and disallow inclusion of arbitrary files. Use secure parser settings and validate all XML input.
